steps.title: Spot the time signal
steps.content: Both Sierra Leone and West Africa experienced urbanization during the same five-year interval (2015–2020, referenced as "that five-year period"). The transition word must express simultaneity.

steps.title: Match the simultaneity
steps.content:
The logical relationship is addition/simultaneity. The blank needs a transition signaling that the West Africa trend happened at the same time as Sierra Leone's.
  • Choice A — "Concurrently,": signals two things happening at the same time, so fits. ✓
  • Choice B — "For example,": signals that the second sentence is a specific instance of the first — but West Africa's trend is a parallel fact, not an example of Sierra Leone's. ✗
  • Choice C — "In other words,": signals a restatement, but the West Africa statistic is a new data point, not a rephrasing of Sierra Leone's. ✗
  • Choice D — "Eventually,": signals something that happens later in time, but the West Africa change occurred in the same 2015–2020 window. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
